description: John Zenon, a 42-year-old warehouse maintenance technician, sustained an acute toxic inhalation injury following a catastrophic anhydrous ammonia leak. He presented with severe respiratory distress, dyspnea, and hypoxia. As the occupational health clinician, complete the industrial chemical inhalation protocol in GNU Health for this patient. Document the toxic inhalation diagnosis (ICD-10 J68.x or T59.x) and perform a clinical evaluation documenting his respiratory distress. Prescribe a respiratory rescue medication, order at least two baseline diagnostic laboratory tests, and schedule a pulmonary follow-up appointment in 7 to 14 days. Login: admin / gnusolidario.
